title: Dungeon Crusade - More Thoughts, KS Reprint, and Give Away
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X22UHszz5Dg

Nine months after the *Uphill Battle to Greatness* review, Daniel (@dungeondive) returns with a short status update on **Dungeon Crusade** — the single thing keeping the game off his table has been fixed.

His original criticism was the *relearn*: the conversational 70-page Crusader's Handbook was tolerable on first read, but months later the prospect of re-reading it just to set the game up was enough to keep the box on the shelf. The new **Quick Reference Guide** — co-written by Roger Pearce and a community member named Ilpo, freely downloadable, soon professionally bound and slipped into reprints and the upgrade kit — solves it. Daniel notes you could very nearly learn the game from the QRG alone, and flags two further additions arriving with the second print run: an **Evolved Minions** variant (a d20 fray-die-style roll with splash damage to adjacent minions) that significantly speeds combat without trivialising it, and a scalable-difficulty card that lowers target numbers for non-combat encounters and traps at hero levels one and two.

The QRG also finally includes setup photographs for each dungeon — an omission from the original release that even attentive followers had been surprised by. He restates his three persistent wishlist items (a real incentive to *trigger* traps, interconnected quest narratives across tables one through three, more interesting hero special-ability progression) and announces a giveaway: Roger is sending him a Crusader of the Realm edition for a Dungeon Dive viewer.

----

How much should the *re*learn cost factor into how you score a game on review? Daniel calls it a make-or-break for shelf longevity — does that match how your collection actually behaves?
